matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 4 > Lunch > Salmon Salad Wrap — Citrus Fresh Style

node.name: Salmon Salad Wrap — Citrus Fresh Style
node.short_description: Protein-packed salmon yogurt salad wrapped in a whole-wheat tortilla with celery and greens.
node.meal_suggestion: Mix salmon with Greek yogurt and diced celery; fill a whole-wheat tortilla with greens to assemble a crunchy, protein-forward wrap. Add a splash of lemon juice for the citrus lift if desired.
node.nutrition_note: Protein-rich wrap with omega-3s from canned salmon and calcium from Greek yogurt. Includes fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and greens plus crunchy celery. Be mindful of sodium in canned salmon; citrus brightness can be added with a squeeze of lemon for the Citrus Fresh Style.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 1 > Lunch > Veggie Quesadilla — Sesame Wrap

node.name: Veggie Quesadilla — Sesame Wrap
node.short_description: A warm veggie quesadilla on a whole-wheat tortilla filled with cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa.
node.meal_suggestion: Pair with a side of fruit or a light yogurt. For more protein in a vegetarian option, add black beans or use bean-based alternative; consider reduced-fat cheddar to lower fat. Optional additions: avocado for healthy fats or a green salad to increase volume and micronutrients.
node.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables; calcium from cheddar; iron from spinach; vitamins A and C from bell pepper. Moderate sodium from cheese and salsa. To reduce fat and calories, use reduced-fat cheddar and a smaller amount of salsa.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 2 > Lunch > Chicken Noodle Soup — Citrus Fresh Twist

node.name: Chicken Noodle Soup — Citrus Fresh Twist
node.short_description: A comforting chicken noodle soup with carrots and celery in broth, offering protein, vegetables, and warmth with a suggested citrus brightness.
node.meal_suggestion: Finish with lemon zest or juice to emphasize the citrus twist; for more fiber, use whole-grain noodles and pair with a small fruit serving for extra vitamin C.
node.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken and carbohydrates from noodles provide balanced nutrition; carrots and celery add fiber and micronutrients; using a light broth helps keep fat moderate. The citrus twist is not shown in the listed ingredients, so lemon would boost vitamin C if added.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 6 > Lunch > Mediterranean Grain Bowl — Garlic-Ginger Twist

node.name: Mediterranean Grain Bowl — Garlic-Ginger Twist
node.short_description: Mediterranean-inspired grain bowl with quinoa, chickpeas, cucumber, tomato, feta, and olive oil, featuring a garlic-ginger flavor twist.
node.meal_suggestion: Mix cooked quinoa with chickpeas, cucumber, and tomato. Top with feta and drizzle with olive oil. Optional: finish with a garlic-ginger twist dressing or a squeeze of lemon and fresh herbs.
node.nutrition_note: Protein- and fiber-rich base from quinoa and chickpeas, plus veggies for hydration. Healthy fats come from feta and olive oil; flavor inspired by a garlic-ginger twist.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 5 > Lunch > Teriyaki Chicken Bowl — Garden Veg Style

node.name: Teriyaki Chicken Bowl — Garden Veg Style
node.short_description: Teriyaki chicken bowl with garden vegetables over rice — a protein-rich, veggie-forward meal.
node.meal_suggestion: A quick, balanced bowl of lean chicken, rice, and mixed vegetables coated in teriyaki glaze. For a lighter version, swap in brown rice and low-sodium sauce to increase fiber and lower sodium.
node.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken; carbohydrates from rice; fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables; teriyaki sauce adds flavor but can be high in sodium; to boost fiber and reduce sodium, use brown rice and/or a reduced-sodium sauce and add more vegetables as desired.
